label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The question is asking how the car tested by Oxford University is different from others. Let's look at the passage again. The first paragraph says that the Oxford team's self-driving car is cheaper because it uses off-the-shelf technology and an ordinary iPad. So "off-the-shelf" means available, low-cost tech. They don't use GPS; instead, the car learns routes when driven by a person. Then it asks if the driver wants to take over. So option B says they depend on GPS, which is actually the opposite of what the passage states. So B is incorrect. Option C says the car can choose places for the owner to visit. The passage mentions the car learns routes to places the driver frequents, like work or the pub. But it doesn't say the car chooses the places; it learns the routes the driver uses. So C is wrong. Option D says the car can develop a good relationship with the owners. The passage doesn't mention anything about relationships. It's about the tech used and how the car learns routes. So D is out. Option A is "use available low-cost technology." The passage says it's cheaper because
